summary: A starter ontology for retirement and pension-scheme modeling, covering scheme participation, sponsorship, contributions, beneficiaries, accounts, and validation rules.

entities.label: Member
entities.description: A person who participates in a pension scheme.
examples: An employee enrolled in a workplace pension scheme.

entities.label: Pension Scheme
entities.description: A retirement arrangement that governs eligibility, contributions, benefits, administration, and payout rules.
examples: A defined contribution workplace pension scheme.

entities.label: Contribution
entities.description: A payment or allocation made into a pension scheme or member account.

entities.label: Employer
entities.description: An organization that employs a member and may sponsor or contribute to a pension scheme.
examples: A company sponsoring a workplace pension scheme.

entities.label: Beneficiary
entities.description: A person or party nominated to receive benefits if a member dies or another triggering event occurs.
examples: A spouse nominated by a member.

entities.label: Account
entities.description: A member-level record of balances, contributions, investments, and transactions.

entities.label: Enrollment Event
entities.description: An event that records a member joining or being enrolled into a pension scheme.
examples: Automatic enrollment into a workplace pension scheme.

relationships.label: belongs to
relationships.description: A member participates in at least one pension scheme.

relationships.label: makes
relationships.description: A member may make one or more contributions to a pension scheme or account.

relationships.label: sponsors
relationships.description: An employer can sponsor a pension scheme for eligible workers.

relationships.label: names
relationships.description: A member can nominate beneficiaries for payable benefits.

relationships.label: is allocated to
relationships.description: A contribution is allocated to a member account.

rules.text: A Contribution must have an amount greater than 0.
rules.rationale: A contribution with a zero or negative amount is not a valid payment into a retirement arrangement.

rules.text: An Enrollment Event must involve a Member who satisfies eligibility criteria.
rules.rationale: Enrollment rules usually require a member to satisfy scheme-specific eligibility criteria.
rules.implementation_hint: Represent scheme-specific eligibility as separate rule entities or SHACL shapes.

rules.text: A Member should name at least one Beneficiary when beneficiary nomination is supported.
rules.rationale: A beneficiary nomination improves benefit administration for death or survivor benefits.
rules.implementation_hint: Make this a configurable business rule because some schemes allow but do not require nominations.

statements.text: A Member belongs to a Pension Scheme.
A Member makes one or more Contributions.
An Employer sponsors a Pension Scheme.
A Contribution is allocated to exactly one Account.
A Contribution must have an amount greater than 0.
statements.predicate: must have an amount greater than

statements.text: An Enrollment Event must involve a Member who satisfies eligibility criteria.
A Member should name at least one Beneficiary when beneficiary nomination is supported.

competency_questions.question: Which pension scheme does a member belong to?
Which employer sponsors a pension scheme?
Which contributions have an amount that is not greater than zero?

assumptions: The draft models a workplace pension scheme at a conceptual level rather than a specific jurisdictional implementation.
Contribution and eligibility rules are draft validation rules and should be checked against the scheme rules and applicable law.
open_questions: Which jurisdiction's pension regulations should define eligibility, auto-enrollment, withdrawal, and benefit rules?
Is the scheme defined contribution, defined benefit, hybrid, or another type?
extension_points: Add Scheme Type, Investment Fund, Benefit, Trustee, Administrator, Vesting Rule, Withdrawal Event, and Retirement Event for a fuller retirement model.
Map validation rules to SHACL shapes after the domain policy decisions are confirmed.
